title: A method for controlling a supply of refrigerant to an evaporator in contingency mode
abstract: A method for controlling a supply of refrigerant to an evaporator (2) of a vapour compression system (1), such as a refrigeration system, an air condition system or a heat pump. During normal operation, the opening degree of the expansion valve (3) is controlled on the basis of an air temperature, T air , of air flowing across the evaporator (2) and/or on the basis of superheat of refrigerant leaving the evaporator (2). In the case that at least one sensor (5, 6, 23, 24) used for obtaining T air or the superheat is malfunctioning, operation of the vapour compression system (1) is switched to a contingency mode. A reference temperature, T out, ref , is calculated, based on previously obtained values of a temperature, T out , of refrigerant leaving the evaporator (2), during a predefined previous time interval, and subsequently the opening degree of the expansion valve (3) is controlled on the basis of the obtained temperature, T out , and in order to reach the calculated reference temperature, T out, ref , of the refrigerant leaving the evaporator (2). Efficient operation of the vapour compression system (1) is obtained, allowing changes in refrigeration load to be taken into account.
claims: Claims CLAI MS 1. A method for controlling a supply of refrigerant to an evaporator (2) of a vapour compression system (1 ), the vapour compression system (1 ) comprising at least one evaporator (2), at least one compressor, at least one condenser and at least one expansion valve (3) arranged in a refrigerant circuit, the method comprising the steps of: obtaining a temperature, Tout, of refrigerant leaving the evaporator (2), obtaining a control parameter value relating to the vapour compression system, controlling an opening degree of the expansion valve (3), on the basis of the obtained control parameter, and in order to reach a reference value for the control parameter, detecting that at least one sensor (5, 6) used for obtaining the control parameter is malfunctioning, calculating a reference temperature, Tout, ret, based on previously obtained values of the temperature, Tout, during a predefined previous time interval, and subsequently controlling the opening degree of the expansion valve (3), on the basis of the obtained temperature, Tout, and in order to reach the calculated reference temperature, Tout, ret, of the refrigerant leaving the evaporator (2). 2. A method according to claim 1 , wherein the step of controlling a control parameter value comprises obtaining a temperature, Tair, of air flowing across the evaporator (2); the step of controlling an opening degree comprises controlling the opening degree of the expansion valve (3), on the basis of the obtained air temperature, Tair, and in order to reach a reference air tern perature, Tair, ret, of the air flowing across the evaporator (2) ; and the step of detecting that at least one sensor (5, 6) is malfunctioning comprises detecting that at least one sensor (5, 6) used for obtaining the air temperature, Tair, is malfunctioning. 3. A method according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the step of calculating a reference temperature, Tout, ret, comprises calculating an average value of obtained values of the temperature, Tout, of refrigerant leaving the evaporator (2) during the predefined previous time interval. 4. A method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the step of subsequently controlling the opening degree of the expansion valve (3) comprises the steps of: providing a perturbation signal, and setting the opening degree of the expansion valve (3) to the controlled opening degree, overlaid with the perturbation signal, - monitoring the temperature, Tout, of refrigerant leaving the evaporator (2), analysing a signal representing the monitored temperature, Tout, and increasing the opening degree of the expansion valve (3) in the case that said analysis reveals that T ou t> Tout, ret. 5. A method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the step of controlling the opening degree of the expansion valve (3), on the basis of the obtained temperature, Tout, and in order to reach the calculated reference temperature, Tout, ret, of the refrigerant leaving the evaporator (2) is continued until the malfunctioning sensor(s) (5, 6) has/have been replaced or repaired. 6. A method according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the step of calculating a reference temperature, Tout, ret, comprises calculating a first reference temperature, Tout, ret, day, based on values of Tout obtained during daytime, and calculating a second reference temperature, Tout, ref, night, based on values of Tout obtained during nighttime.
